commit.message:
Fix WASM effects: Embind base class binding for process()

Root cause: EXPORT_WASM_EFFECT macro registered derived classes without
declaring the WASMEffectBase relationship. Embind silently dropped methods
defined only in the base class (process, isInitialized, getSampleRate,
getParameterName). The worklet called effect.process() which was undefined,
caught by try/catch and fell through to passthrough mode — ALL 6 WASM
effects were silently passing audio through unprocessed.

Fix: Register WASMEffectBase as a proper Embind base class with all methods,
then derive effect classes with emscripten::base<WASMEffectBase>. Also changed
processJS signature from uintptr_t to unsigned int for Embind type resolution.

Rebuilt all 6 affected WASM effects:
- SpringReverb (confirmed params now affect audio in Node.js test)
- MVerb
- Leslie
- MoogFilters
- ShimmerReverb
- GranularFreeze

Also:
- Removed temporary FX-DEBUG console.warn logs
- MasterEffectsPanel: replaced FULL_WET_EFFECTS with getDefaultEffectWet()
- fx-audit.ts: comprehensive rewrite with knob testing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix COM reentrancy contract violations and re-enable the ExtensionPoints test (#126571)

This PR fixes the `Interop/COM/ExtensionPoints` failure by correcting
CoreCLR COM interop contract handling for paths that can legitimately
re-enter managed code during COM allocation hooks, and it includes a
small cleanup of the related COM visibility helpers. Now that the test
infrastructure change has merged, it also re-enables the
`ExtensionPoints` IMallocSpy test.

## Changes

- Keep `OleVariant::ClearLPSTRArray` and `OleVariant::ClearLPWSTRArray`
as `NOTHROW`, but mark the `CoTaskMemFree` cleanup paths with a
permanent `ThrowsViolation` for runtime reentrancy, since
`CoTaskMemFree` can invoke a managed `IMallocSpy` implementation.
- Wrap the `ComPreStubWorker` setup around the managed exception
dispatcher and unwind handler in a
`BEGIN_CONTRACT_VIOLATION(ThrowsViolation)` / `END_CONTRACT_VIOLATION`
block.
- Update the outer static contract on `ComPreStubWorker` to
`STATIC_CONTRACT_NOTHROW` so it matches the intended HRESULT-returning
behavior outside the explicit violation scope.
- Remove the unused `fForIDispatch` parameter from
`CheckParentComVisibility`.
- Delete the redundant `CheckParentComVisibilityNoThrow` helper and use
`HasInvisibleParent()` directly at the remaining visibility check.
- Re-enable `src/tests/Interop/COM/ExtensionPoints/ExtensionPoints.cs`
by removing the temporary `ActiveIssue` skip on
`Validate_Managed_IMallocSpy`.

## Why this addresses the failure

The failing test exercises COM extension points that can callback into
managed code during allocation cleanup and stub initialization. Those
paths were operating under overly strict contracts, which made valid
reentrant behavior show up as contract violations. This change narrows
the violation handling to the specific reentrant regions while keeping
the surrounding HRESULT-returning contracts intact. The
visibility-helper refactors are supporting cleanups with no intended
behavioral change, and re-enabling the test ensures the original failure
scenario is covered again now that the test can run.

fix: pre-create all TLD partitions at worker startup to prevent DDL/DML lock deadlock

Running CREATE TABLE ... PARTITION OF domain during the hot-path (concurrent
with INSERT batches from another container) requires ACCESS EXCLUSIVE on the
parent domain table, which queued behind ROW EXCLUSIVE locks from ongoing
INSERTs — causing a full deadlock that stalled both containers.

Fix: _prewarm_all_partitions() runs at main() startup before run_sync_cycle(),
creating any missing partitions while no concurrent ingest is active. From that
point, ensure_partition() inside the per-TLD hot-path is always a no-op
(partition already exists), so no DDL ever contends with live INSERT batches.

commit.message:
Introduce LedgerSeq newtype for ledger sequence numbers

Replace raw u32 with a LedgerSeq newtype wrapper across the entire workspace.
The type provides type safety at ledger sequence boundaries while maintaining
ergonomic arithmetic and comparison operations.

Key design decisions:
- Private inner field with explicit get()/new() for XDR boundary conversions
- From<u32>/Into<u32> for ergonomic conversions
- Arithmetic ops (Add/Sub/Rem with u32) to avoid .get() in common patterns
- PartialEq<u32>/PartialOrd<u32> for direct comparison
- Serde transparent, rusqlite ToSql/FromSql behind feature flag
- No Div (use .get() first), no tracing::Value (sealed trait)

113 files changed across all workspace crates.

Closes #1226

Fix view toggle missing from mobile overflow menu on iOS

The card/list view toggle was not rendering in the SecondaryActions
dropdown on MAUI Blazor Hybrid (iOS WebView). Root cause: inline
ternary expressions in Razor markup (@onclick and @() class/text
bindings) caused the MAUI WebView Blazor renderer to silently drop
the entire <li> element from the render tree.

Fix: extract the ternary logic into computed properties
(ViewToggleIcon, ViewToggleLabel) and a ToggleViewMode method,
keeping the markup simple enough for the WebView renderer.

the menu now shows all items including 'List View' toggle.
